regression_guards: tldr-daemon crate (all pass incl. val004 OnceCell)

red_evidence.snippet: panicked at crates/tldr-cli/tests/val013_daemon_status_cross_cwd_test.rs:143:5: assertion left == right failed: ... reported status="not_running", but the daemon IS alive ... Expected status="running". Full stdout: { "status": "not_running", "message": "Daemon not running" }

green_evidence.test_run: running 2 tests; test daemon_status_from_other_cwd_reports_running ... ok; test daemon_status_with_explicit_project_still_works_from_other_cwd ... ok; test result: ok. 2 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

implementation_notes: New module crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon_active.rs encapsulates the discovery file (write_active, read_active, remove_active, is_pid_alive). Atomic writes use tmp-then-rename to avoid half-written files.
is_pid_alive uses kill(pid, 0); EPERM (foreign-uid daemon) is treated as alive, ESRCH as dead.
daemon status only consults the discovery fallback when args.project == Path::new(".") — explicit --project is always honored. This preserves the workaround path as a regression guard.

daemon-active.json is removed on stop in all three success paths (already not running, normal shutdown, daemon already stopped). The foreground daemon's run_foreground also calls remove_active after daemon.run() returns, mirroring cleanup_socket.

If dirs::cache_dir() is unavailable, falls back to ./.cache/tldr/daemon-active.json. Write failures are non-fatal (eprintln warning); status falls back to legacy cwd behavior. Multi-daemon case is unchanged — users still pass --project explicitly. Global daemon registry deferred to v0.3.0 per VAL-013 spec.
